title: Local domain strength without registry-wide exception status
scenario: `sol-trading-001` can read as highly useful inside one domain and may tempt an exceptional interpretation, but its usefulness remains condition-sensitive and domain-bound rather than registry-wide in the strong exception sense.
signals_for_escalation: domain users may experience the card as unusually useful in the local setting
specialized heuristics can look stronger than they really are when they fit one context well
apparent local leverage can be mistaken for registry-wide exceptional value
signals_against_escalation: the card is explicitly condition-sensitive rather than broadly transferable
current confidence is already restrained rather than near-certain
the case shows bounded domain fit, not a general registry-level exception pattern
why_no_material_escalation: The case does not justify a new escalation because the main signal is contextual usefulness, not a broader unresolved inflation or exception problem across the registry.
why_not_s2_entry_relevant: A domain-conditioned solution does not show that S1 lacks a necessary interpretive axis. It shows that local usefulness and registry-wide exception status must stay distinct under the existing reading discipline.
why_not_adjacent_escalation_readings: This is not a soft-flag-worthy inflation case because the issue is not exaggerated certainty. It is not a justified exception in the stronger sense because the local usefulness does not expand into registry-wide exception force. It is not a borderline S2 case because no missing model layer is exposed.
notes: Use this case to keep local utility from being over-read as general exception strength.
